message:
🐛 Fix Kyverno mission: use chart 3.6.3 for K8s < 1.32 compatibility (#378)

Chart 3.7.1 (app v1.17.1) requires MutatingAdmissionPolicy v1alpha1
which is only available in Kubernetes 1.32+. On K8s 1.25-1.31 (including
OpenShift 4.14-4.18), the admission controller TLS secret is never
generated, leaving pods permanently Not Ready.

- Downgrade from chart 3.7.1 to 3.6.3 (app v1.16.3)
- Add version compatibility note explaining the K8s 1.32 requirement
- Add troubleshooting for TLS secret not created on older K8s
- Add troubleshooting for reports controller OOMKilled

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@
       },
       {
         "title": "Install Kyverno using Helm",
-        "description": "Next, install Kyverno in your Kubernetes cluster with the following command. This will create the necessary namespace and install Kyverno v1.17.1:
```bash
helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.7.1
```

**Note:** The chart version is `3.7.1` (app version v1.17.1). Do not use `--version v1.17.1` — that is the app version, not the chart version.

**OpenShift clusters:** Kyverno pods require Security Context Constraints (SCCs). After install, grant `nonroot-v2` SCC to all Kyverno service accounts:
```bash
for sa in kyverno-admission-controller kyverno-background-controller kyverno-cleanup-controller kyverno-reports-controller; do
  kubectl create clusterrolebinding "kyverno-scc-${sa}" --clusterrole=system:openshift:scc:nonroot-v2 --serviceaccount="kyverno:${sa}"
done
kubectl -n kyverno rollout restart deployment kyverno-admission-controller kyverno-background-controller kyverno-cleanup-controller kyverno-reports-controller
```"
+        "description": "Next, install Kyverno in your Kubernetes cluster with the following command. This will create the necessary namespace and install Kyverno v1.16.3:
```bash
helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.6.3
```

**Note:** The chart version is `3.6.3` (app version v1.16.3). Do not use `--version v1.16.3` — that is the app version, not the chart version.

**Version compatibility:** Chart `3.7.1` (v1.16.3) requires Kubernetes 1.32+ due to its dependency on the `MutatingAdmissionPolicy` v1alpha1 API. For Kubernetes 1.25–1.31 (including OpenShift 4.14–4.18), use chart `3.6.3` (v1.16.3).

**OpenShift clusters:** Kyverno pods require Security Context Constraints (SCCs). After install, grant `nonroot-v2` SCC to all Kyverno service accounts:
```bash
for sa in kyverno-admission-controller kyverno-background-controller kyverno-cleanup-controller kyverno-reports-controller; do
  kubectl create clusterrolebinding "kyverno-scc-${sa}" --clusterrole=system:openshift:scc:nonroot-v2 --serviceaccount="kyverno:${sa}"
done
kubectl -n kyverno rollout restart deployment kyverno-admission-controller kyverno-background-controller kyverno-cleanup-controller kyverno-reports-controller
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify the installation",
@@ -35,7 +35,7 @@
       "summary": "A successful installation of Kyverno will show the Kyverno pods in a running state when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace kyverno`. You can also verify the configuration by applying a sample policy and checking for enforcement.",
       "codeSnippets": [
         "helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno
helm repo update",
-        "helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.7.1",
+        "helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.6.3",
         "kubectl get pods --namespace kyverno",
         "helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --set podSecurityStandard=baseline",
         "apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-non-root
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: require-non-root
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Run as non-root user"
        pattern:
          spec:
            securityContext:
              runAsUser: 1000"
@@ -85,6 +85,14 @@
       {
         "title": "Namespace not found error",
         "description": "If you receive an error indicating that the namespace 'kyverno' is not found, ensure that the namespace exists and is correctly specified in your commands:
```bash
kubectl get namespaces
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Admission controller TLS secret not created (K8s < 1.32)",
+        "description": "If the admission controller pod stays Not Ready with repeated `TLS handshake error: secret kyverno-svc.kyverno.svc.kyverno-tls-pair not found` messages, you are likely running Kyverno chart 3.7.1+ on Kubernetes < 1.32. Chart 3.7.1 (app v1.17.1) requires the `MutatingAdmissionPolicy` v1alpha1 API which is only available in Kubernetes 1.32+. The missing API blocks TLS certificate generation.

**Fix:** Downgrade to chart 3.6.3 (app v1.16.3):
```bash
helm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno --no-hooks
kubectl -n kyverno delete secret -l owner=helm,name=kyverno
helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.6.3
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Reports controller OOMKilled",
+        "description": "The Kyverno reports controller defaults to 128Mi memory limit, which may be insufficient for clusters with many workloads. If the pod shows OOMKilled, increase its memory:
```bash
kubectl -n kyverno patch deployment kyverno-reports-controller --type=json -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources/limits/memory","value":"512Mi"},{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources/requests/memory","value":"256Mi"}]'
```"
       }
     ]
   },
@@ -111,9 +119,9 @@
       "helm"
     ],
     "maturity": "incubating",
-    "projectVersion": "v1.17.1",
+    "projectVersion": "v1.16.3",
     "containerImages": [
-      "ghcr.io/kyverno/kyverno:v1.17.1"
+      "ghcr.io/kyverno/kyverno:v1.16.3"
     ],
     "sourceUrls": {
       "docs": "https://kyverno.io",
